commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d439ef26da7bea2b1ea59c5d042a81a9daad36a7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Qwen/DashScope image capability guard across media-understanding selection/runtime paths, switches the Qwen image default to `qwen3.5-plus`, keeps compatible-mode image serialization on `image_url`, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +92, Tests +222. Total +314 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main trusts configured Qwen image metadata in multiple image selection paths, and the linked issue plus PR live output show DashScope rejects `qwen3.7-max` image input. I did not run a live OpenClaw gateway session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is needed because the remaining blockers involve provider ownership/default policy plus a simple lint repair, not a safe standalone automation fix.

target.title: fix #92688: [Bug]: Qwen vision models fail with 400 "Unexpected item type in content" on DashScope
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes Qwen/DashScope image understanding routing when user config incorrectly marks known text-only Qwen max chat models as image-capable.
- Adds a shared media capability guard so configured model metadata cannot override known provider/model facts for image support.
- Routes Qwen image fallback to the provider image default (`qwen3.5-plus`) and keeps DashScope compatible payloads on the OpenAI `image_url` shape.
- Intentionally does not switch DashScope compatible chat completions to the native `{ type: "image" }` block shape, because live DashScope checks reject that shape for image-capable Qwen models.
- Success means `qwen/qwen3.7-max` is no longer selected for image understanding, while Qwen image-capable defaults still receive valid OpenAI-compatible image payloads.

## Linked context

Closes #92688

Related #92704

This was not requested by a maintainer. #92704 is an existing related PR, but local live proof showed the issue is model capability routing rather than DashScope needing native image content blocks.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Qwen/DashScope image tool calls could route to `qwen3.7-max`, which DashScope reports as text-only for image input.
- Real environment tested: DashScope OpenAI-compatible chat completions with a local temporary API key; secret value was not logged.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent redacted image chat-completion probes to DashScope compatible mode with Qwen image-capable models and the problematic `qwen3.7-max` model. Then ran the focused regression suite listed in Tests and validation.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  Redacted DashScope live output, compatible-mode endpoint, API key not logged:

  qwen3.5-plus + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 200, assistant text: 'red'
  qwen3.7-plus + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 200, assistant text: 'red'
  qwen-vl-max + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 200, assistant text: 'red'

  qwen3.7-max + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 400
  Error preview: Unexpected item type in content

  qwen3.7-max + native image shape: HTTP 400
  Error preview: The current model only supports text modality and does not support image input.

  DashScope native image block shape with image-capable Qwen models: HTTP 400
  Error preview: Invalid value: image. Supported values are: 'text','image_url','video_url' and 'video'.
  ```

- Observed result after fix: Live DashScope output confirms `qwen3.7-max` is not a usable image model, while `qwen3.5-plus` accepts the OpenAI-compatible `image_url` payload and returns the expected image answer. The regression suite confirms OpenClaw now rejects/skips `qwen/qwen3.7-max` for image understanding even when config marks it as `text,image`, and Qwen image fallback resolves to `qwen/qwen3.5-plus`.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full interactive OpenClaw gateway session in this environment, and did not test every Qwen model alias available in DashScope.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Screenshots were not captured; the evidence is copied terminal output plus redacted live DashScope response summaries. One live DashScope model (`qwen-vl-max-latest`) returned `403 access_denied` under the temporary key, so the default was moved to an accessible image-capable model.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

  ```text
  qwen3.7-max + OpenAI-compatible image_url shape: HTTP 400
  Error preview: Unexpected item type in content

  qwen3.7-max + native image shape: HTTP 400
  Error preview: The current model only supports text modality and does not support image input.

  DashScope native image block shape with image-capable Qwen models: HTTP 400
  Error preview: Invalid value: image. Supported values are: 'text','image_url','video_url' and 'video'.
  ```

## Maintainer-ready notes

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix. This PR changes image-model capability resolution for known Qwen Cloud text-only chat models instead of adding retry logic, masking the provider rejection, or changing DashScope payload syntax.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High after focused regression coverage and live DashScope compatible-mode proof. The remaining decision is maintainer judgment on the related PR #92704 and whether this scoped provider-fact guard is the preferred canonical shape.
- Root cause: Qwen/DashScope model metadata from user config could mark `qwen3.7-max` as image-capable, and multiple image selection paths trusted that metadata over provider/model facts. That allowed OpenClaw to select a text-only Qwen chat model for image input, so DashScope rejected the request with 400 instead of running an image-capable Qwen model.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The invariant is that configured model metadata must not override known provider/model modality facts. The new shared guard makes `qwen3.7-max` non-image for Qwen Cloud provider aliases, then applies that source-of-truth check at configured discovery, runner fallback, image-tool config, PDF image fallback, and runtime execution. The DashScope wire format stays OpenAI-compatible `image_url` because live proof shows image-capable Qwen models accept that shape and reject native `{ type: "image" }` blocks.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who set Qwen as their primary model can upload images or use PDF/image fallback without OpenClaw routing those image requests into a Qwen text-only max chat model that fails immediately with DashScope 400 responses.
- What did NOT change: This does not change the DashScope compatible endpoint, does not switch to native image content blocks, does not broaden Qwen model alias handling beyond the known non-image `qwen3.7-max` family, and does not affect non-Qwen providers.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: Provider/model capability facts are the canonical boundary for known unsupported modalities, while user config remains preference metadata. Related PR #92704 was considered, but live DashScope proof indicates the issue is model capability routing rather than a compatible-mode protocol migration.
- Target test file: Regression coverage is in `src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts`, `extensions/qwen/index.test.ts`, and `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: Tests lock the active-model fallback from `qwen/qwen3.7-max` to `qwen/qwen3.5-plus`, image-tool and PDF fallback config skipping mislabelled Qwen max metadata, runtime rejection before provider completion, Qwen provider image defaults, and DashScope compatible `image_url` payload shape.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The guard is centralized but used at every image-model selection boundary, so later callers cannot accidentally re-trust mislabelled config. The test-only `as unknown as OpenClawConfig` keeps the regression fixture narrow, and the PDF test name uses "fallback" to describe the expected image fallback behavior, not a production fallback workaround.
- Patch quality notes: Patch quality warnings are expected for auth-provider/compatibility/default surfaces because this PR touches provider routing and Qwen image defaults. The diff spans several entrypoints to enforce one invariant consistently, while staying out of unrelated refactors, schema changes, migrations, and non-Qwen providers.
- Risk labels considered: auth-provider, compatibility, public_config.
- Risk explanation: The highest-risk area is classifying Qwen model capability and changing the Qwen image default to `qwen3.5-plus` when `qwen-vl-max-latest` returned `403 access_denied` under the live test key.
- Why acceptable: The guard is narrowly scoped to Qwen Cloud provider aliases and `qwen3.7-max` model IDs, and the live proof plus regression suite cover both the rejected text-only model and accepted image-capable Qwen compatible-mode payloads.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts src/media-understanding/image.test.ts src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts extensions/qwen/index.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- Added/updated regression coverage for:
  - active Qwen text-only model falling back to provider image default;
  - image tool model config not selecting configured `qwen3.7-max` image metadata;
  - PDF image fallback not using mislabelled Qwen max chat metadata;
  - runtime image execution rejecting known non-image Qwen models before provider completion;
  - DashScope-compatible image payload staying on OpenAI `image_url` shape.
- The runtime guard test failed before the fix because completion was called with `qwen3.7-max` instead of rejecting it.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, Qwen image defaults and configured provider model interpretation change for known non-image Qwen max chat models.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is provider/model capability classification for Qwen aliases.

How is that risk mitigated?

The guard is scoped to Qwen Cloud provider aliases and `qwen3.7-max` model IDs, and tests cover runner fallback, image tool config, PDF fallback config, runtime execution, Qwen provider defaults, and OpenAI-compatible DashScope payload shape.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Still waiting on: CI and maintainer judgment on the known related PR #92704. This PR intentionally keeps the compatible-mode `image_url` request shape because live DashScope proof showed native `{ type: "image" }` blocks are rejected.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
